summary: Prose-coverage walk per file_schemas.md § Coverage-Required Fields finds that IP1.default_branch, IP2.default_branch, IP3.default_branch, and IP4.default_branch in decisions/discovery/ledger-v1.json have no assertions with a 'covers' field pointing at them directly. Coverage IS satisfied transitively (IPn.chosen_branch in sections-v1.json is itself coverage-required and is covered by machine_checkable_assertions), and IP5/IP6 default_branch is covered directly. The strict property check fails for IP1-IP4 default_branch.

evidence.minor_anomaly: DCA.FC.3 has covers='decisions/discovery/ledger-v1.json#first_contact_requirements[id=FC.3]' (omits '.description' suffix); all other FC DCAs use '.description'. Resolves to the same prose, but inconsistent with the coverage-required canonical path.

options: Codify in file_schemas.md that ledger#inflection_points[id=IPn].default_branch is satisfied transitively when sections-v1.json#inflection_resolutions[ip_id=IPn].chosen_branch is covered (since chosen_branch IS the resolution of default_branch).

Require TD to add explicit DCA entries that cover ledger#inflection_points[].default_branch alongside the existing chosen_branch coverage. Adds N more entries but makes the property check trivially satisfiable.
Normalize covers-pointer suffix conventions so the strict walker can resolve them without alias rules (e.g., always end with the canonical field name as listed in the coverage-required table).

rationale_for_severity: Cross-section-architectural: this is a schema-vs-implementation property mismatch, not a section-level defect. The underlying decisions ARE covered; the schema's strict reading just isn't met. v1.10 candidate.
